result: {"verdict":"ACCEPT","blocking":[],"nonblocking":[{"id":"NB1","area":"stream-teardown-edge","note":"In open_stream, the non-2xx branch calls `await stream_cm.__aexit__(None,None,None)` manually but leaves `stream_entered=True`. If `response.aread()` or that `__aexit__` itself raises an httpx.TimeoutException/TransportError, the outer except handler will call `__aexit__` again (guarded only by stream_entered), producing a double-exit on an already-torn-down stream context. httpx makes response.aclose() effectively idempotent so this is not a leak or premature-client-close, but resetting `stream_entered=False` immediately after the manual exit would make the guard precise. Advisory only."},{"id":"NB2","area":"cache-key-scope","note":"Cache key is `(name, base_url, model)` while the spec says 'cached by upstream id'. Because Phase 1 enforces unique upstream names, this is a harmless superset key that still yields exactly one client per upstream; the extra components only defend against a name collision that Phase 1 already prevents. No change required."},{"id":"NB3","area":"config-provenance","note":"connect/write/pool timeout fields on Upstream are consumed by default_client_factory but config.py is not in this diff; the new default-connect==5.0 assertion in test_default_client_factory_uses_split_timeouts plus green full gate (31 passed, Ruff clean) confirm the 5s default resolves. Confirmed adequately by tests/evidence."}],"required_patches":[],"certification_statement":"Phase 2 client-pooling is certified ACCEPT. The three prior nonblocking notes are validly patched: (1) a new streaming pooling test (test_streaming_reuses_pooled_clients_until_router_close) asserts the factory is invoked once per upstream across sequential streaming requests and that pooled clients stay open (closed==[]) until router.aclose(); (2) test_default_client_factory_uses_split_timeouts now asserts the default connect timeout is 5.0 alongside split read/write/pool values; (3) the client cache key was changed from name-only to (name, base_url, model), removing accidental-collision risk. Correctness holds across the review surface: RichardRouter caches one httpx.AsyncClient per upstream via _client_for keyed through the injected factory; chat_completion and open_stream both reuse the pooled client with the per-attempt aclose() removed, eliminating premature closes; aclose() snapshots-clears-then-closes, and _stream_iterator closes only the stream context (not the client), so the success, non-2xx, timeout, and transport-error paths no longer close pooled clients and the stream_entered guard prevents __aexit__ on an un-entered context. create_app gains an optional client_factory and registers a FastAPI lifespan that awaits router.aclose() on shutdown, preserving create_app/app/CLI back-compat. Tests cover factory-once-per-upstream, streaming reuse, split timeouts, 5s connect default, and lifespan close; local gates report targeted 13 passed and full 31 passed with Ruff clean and failover tests unchanged/green. No accidental recursion, no lifespan unsafety, and no scope drift into Phase 3 breaker or Phase 4 decision-log behavior. The remaining notes (non-2xx double-__aexit__ edge, superset cache key, config.py out-of-diff) are advisory and do not gate acceptance."}
